news.title: Argentina projected lineup for World Cup final vs. Spain: Who will play with Messi?
news.lede: Lionel Messi and Argentina have one more match to win to become back-to-back World Cup champions. They face Spain in the 2026 World Cup final on Sunday, July 19 at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey. Messi has been the star of the tournament. He leads the Golden Boot race with eight goals and broke the record for most World Cup goals all-time.

news.title: 40-year long Bayern Munich World Cup streak ended by cowardly England losing at the end
news.lede: Since 1982, Bayern Munich have had at least one player in the final of every single World Cup. With both England and France now eliminated in the semi-inal, Harry Kane, Michael Olise, and Dayot Upamecano have all exited the tournament. Just to recap, here is the incredible streak that Bayern Munich have maintained all these years, most of them coming courtesy of strong German performances at the biggest stage.

news.title: AI predictions for Spain vs. Argentina World Cup final
news.lede: Argentina is looking to become the first team to win back-to-back World Cups since Brazil in 1958 and 1962. To achieve that goal, Lionel Messi's squad will have to beat Spain in the 2026 World Cup final on Sunday, July 19. Spain has enjoyed a quality performance at the 2026 World Cup to date. La Roja have only conceded one goal and are coming off a convincing, 2-0 win over the tournament favorites, France, during which their squad made Kylian Mbappé and the French offense uncomfortable.

news.title: World Cup 2026: England 1-2 Argentina - Three Lions suffer semi-final loss after stunning late turnaround by holders
news.lede: England missed out on their first World Cup final since 1966 after a stunning late turnaround from rivals Argentina saw the holders earn a 2-1 victory thanks to Lautaro Martinez's stoppage-time winner. The Three Lions were leading 1-0 with five minutes of normal time remaining in Atlanta through Anthony Gordon's early second-half opener. But two goals in the space of seven minutes saw a remarkable comeback from Argentina as Enzo Fernandez levelled with a wonderful 85th-minute strike from distance before substitute Martinez headed in a 92nd-minute winner. But following the changes, Argentina took firm control of the game and deservedly came back, with Lionel Messi playing a huge influence as he assisted both goals, producing a fine cross for Martinez's winner.

news.title: Romero’s World Cup heroics for Argentina make him one of Messi’s most trusted
news.lede: The mercurial performance at the back was a far cry from the gaffe-prone Spurs captain we have become accustomed to seeing. As Cristian Romero readied to receive a pass 10 yards from his goalline, Jude Bellingham and Anthony Gordon sensed a mistake in the making. The center-back’s posture was not ideal after he took a scoot backwards to collect, facing the left sideline as his opponents lustily converged. He would only have half a second to settle a bouncing ball, shift his momentum, and find a passing lane in the rapidly closing seam between the England duo.

news.title: Spain at the World Cup 2026: Squad, fixtures, group and history
news.lede: Everything you need to know about Spain at the 2026 World Cup: their fixtures, squad, qualifying record, key players, coach and tournament history. European champions Spain are looking to complete an international double at FIFA World Cup 2026. Luis de la Fuente's side, led by captain Rodri, topped Group H after drawing with Cabo Verde before beating Saudi Arabia and Uruguay. They then eliminated Austria in the round of 32, Portugal in the last 16, Belgium in the quarter-finals and France in the semi-finals.

Spain will face Argentina in the World Cup 2026 final. La Roja became the first team to book their place in this weekend’s tournament showpiece with a 2-0 win over France in Arlington, Texas.

news.title: The bigger picture: Tuchel gets blame for England exit but this is not only about him | Barney Ronay
news.lede: German will be pilloried for his semi-final decisions but that is to ignore the fact English football culture is not set up to win major tournaments. I wanted it to be you so badly.” As England’s World Cup hopes recede into another spell of heartache, let down in Atlanta by the latest handsome, cadaverous Mr Right, a little sadder, not much wiser, sunburnt, broke, eating Jägerbomb ice cream out of the tub with a spoon, this is a good moment to seek some classic New York romcom solace. Don’t be sad that it’s over.
